world.title: Iran executes man as January protest crackdown continues
world.summary: Iran executed a man on Tuesday for allegedly setting fire to a mosque and engaging in anti-security activities, linked to protests earlier this year. The judiciary announced the execution amid ongoing crackdowns on dissent. In related news, sections of the Bagh Rezvan cemetery in Rasht have been cemented over to create a uniform surface above the graves of slain protestors.

world.title: Kirpans out, turbans tossed in air: Multiple injured in violent clash in Germany gurdwara
world.summary: A violent clash at a gurdwara in Germany resulted in multiple injuries, prompting the deployment of nearly 100 police officers, including a Special Task Force and a helicopter for aerial monitoring. Witnesses reported the use of pepper spray and a firearm, along with knives. The incident occurred shortly before a prayer service, leaving attendees in shock.

business.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
business.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added eight exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. This includes three flaws affecting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager. CISA has set deadlines for federal agencies to address these vulnerabilities by April 23 and May 4, 2026, emphasizing the urgency for federal patching efforts.

business.title: New Hyundai Ioniq 3 Debuts - 496 Kms Range, 4.1m Long, 400V Architecture
business.summary: Hyundai has introduced the Ioniq 3 all-electric vehicle at Milan Design Week 2026. The Ioniq 3, measuring 4.1 meters in length, features a range of 496 kilometers and operates on a 400V architecture. Production will take place at Hyundai’s Izmit facility in Turkey, with plans for the vehicle to launch in European markets later this year.

business.title: Gold struggles near daily low on firmer USD, ahead of US
business.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) remains below the $4,800 mark during the early European session on Tuesday, despite being above a one-week low reached previously. The metal is facing intraday losses, influenced by a stronger US dollar. Investor sentiment is cautious regarding a potential agreement between the US and Iran amid ongoing tensions over the Strait of Hormuz.

technology.title: Apple names Johny Srouji hardware chief as John Ternus prepares to take over as CEO
technology.summary: Apple has appointed Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer, overseeing the company's hardware division. This change comes as John Ternus prepares to assume the role of chief executive officer later this year. Tim Cook praised Srouji for his influence on Apple's silicon strategy, while Ternus expressed intentions to collaborate closely with Srouji during the leadership transition.

technology.title: Poco M8s 5G Launched With 7,000mAh Battery, 50-Megapixel Camera: Price, Specifications
technology.summary: Poco M8s 5G has been launched globally, featuring a 7,000mAh battery and a 50-megapixel camera. This smartphone is part of Poco's budget M lineup and will be available for purchase through Xiaomi's website in select markets. The device aims to offer enhanced performance and features at an affordable price, catering to budget-conscious consumers.

technology.title: Google Drive Desktop 2026: Ransomware Detection & Restore Explained
technology.summary: As of 2026, Google Drive Desktop introduces ransomware detection and file restoration features for subscribers. The ransomware detection actively scans files for encryption and pauses syncing if a threat is identified. Users can restore files to previous versions if they are affected by ransomware. These enhancements aim to improve data security and user confidence in file management on the platform.

sports.title: Cricket Fixing? Chilling Link Between Lawrence Bishnoi Gang And Canada's T20 World Cup Campaign
sports.summary: The International Cricket Council (ICC) is investigating a fixing scandal linked to Canada’s T20 World Cup campaign. Allegations involve the team’s captain, Dilpreet Bajwa, and connections to the Lawrence Bishnoi gang. Reports indicate potential manipulation of matches during the tournament, raising concerns about the integrity of the sport. The investigation aims to uncover the extent of these allegations.

sports.title: Mumbai Indians Called Out For "Commercial" Hardik Pandya Captaincy Call: "Seniors Didn't Support Him"
sports.summary: Hardik Pandya assumed captaincy of Mumbai Indians from Rohit Sharma in 2024, facing significant criticism. The team struggled initially, losing four of their first five matches. However, they achieved a decisive 99-run victory against Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026, alleviating some pressure. Concerns were raised about senior players not supporting Pandya during his challenging start as captain.

sports.title: Sanjay Manjrekar plays down hype around Hardik Pandya's captaincy: ‘He was only brilliant because he had Ashish Nehra’
sports.summary: Sanjay Manjrekar has downplayed the excitement surrounding Hardik Pandya's captaincy, suggesting that his success was largely due to the support of Ashish Nehra. He criticized the Mumbai Indians for appointing Pandya as captain without sufficient experience. In 2024, the team finished last, facing fan backlash, but improved in 2025 by reaching Qualifier 2 in the tournament.

health.title: Moringa seeds can help remove microplastics from drinking water, says study
health.summary: Scientists in Brazil discovered that moringa seeds can effectively remove microplastics from drinking water. The seeds cause the tiny plastic particles to clump together, facilitating their filtration. This method is cost-effective and utilizes a readily available natural resource, potentially offering a sustainable solution to address microplastic contamination in water sources. Further research may enhance its practical applications.

health.title: Personalised vaccine shows lasting promise against deadly pancreatic cancer
health.summary: A Phase 1 trial of a personalised mRNA vaccine for early-stage pancreatic cancer involved 16 patients post-surgery. Results after six years indicated that nearly all patients who responded to the vaccine remain alive. This promising outcome suggests a potential new approach in treating pancreatic cancer, which is known for its high mortality rate and limited treatment options.

health.title: Intermittent Fasting Can Boost Longer Life
health.source: Asharq Al-Awsat | Explore World News Today
health.summary: A study in Nature Communications indicates that intermittent fasting may enhance longevity, based on research conducted on small worms. Scientists from the University of Texas Southwestern Medical Center compared normally fed worms to those subjected to a 24-hour fast in early adulthood. They assessed factors like stored fat, gene activity related to fat metabolism, and lifespan outcomes.

science.title: Neanderthal-Human hybrid theory debunked as suspect skull is reidentified
science.summary: A prehistoric skull fragment from Hahnöfersand, Germany, initially believed to be evidence of a Neanderthal-human hybrid, has been reidentified as a standard modern human. Discovered in 1973 and dated to approximately 36,000 years ago, the frontal bone's reclassification challenges previous theories regarding interbreeding between Neanderthals and Homo sapiens, altering the understanding of human evolution.

science.title: Bathtub ring on Mars hints at ancient ocean that covered one-third of Red Planet
science.summary: Scientists from the California Institute of Technology have identified a geological feature on Mars, referred to as a "bathtub ring," which suggests the presence of a vast, ancient ocean covering one-third of the planet. This discovery provides strong evidence that Mars once hosted long-lasting liquid water, contributing to the ongoing investigation of its geological history and potential habitability.

science.title: Bathtub ring on Mars hints at ancient ocean that covered one-third of Red Planet
science.summary: Scientists from Caltech have identified a geological feature on Mars, termed a "bathtub ring," suggesting the presence of a vast, ancient ocean that may have covered one-third of the planet. This discovery provides significant evidence that Mars once hosted long-lasting liquid water, moving beyond the previous understanding of only lakes and rivers existing on the Red Planet.

entertainment.title: Is Allu Arjun Moving to Mumbai from Hyderabad? Allu Aravind Reacts
entertainment.summary: Rumors have surfaced about Allu Arjun relocating from Hyderabad to Mumbai, with some reports suggesting that Sun Pictures declined to cover flight expenses, influencing this decision. Allu Aravind, Allu Arjun's father, has responded to these speculations, dismissing them as unfounded. The situation has sparked interest within the Telugu cinema community, but official confirmation regarding any move remains unannounced.

entertainment.title: Riteish Deshmukh and Genelia Deshmukh's 10-year-old son Rahyl steals spotlight with his debut in ‘Raja Shivaji’ trailer - Watch
entertainment.summary: The trailer for 'Raja Shivaji' was launched in Mumbai, marking the debut of Riteish Deshmukh and Genelia Deshmukh's 10-year-old son, Rahyl, as young Shivaji Maharaj. The event was a family affair, featuring Genelia as Saibai, alongside a star-studded cast including Sanjay Dutt and Abhishek Bachchan. Riteish expressed gratitude during this emotional occasion.

entertainment.title: Ayushmann Khurrana, Sharvari starrer Yeh Prem Mol Liya gets November 27, 2026 theatrical release
entertainment.summary: Filmmaker Sooraj R. Barjatya has announced his upcoming film Yeh Prem Mol Liya, featuring Ayushmann Khurrana and Sharvari. The movie's theatrical release is scheduled for November 27, 2026. Produced by Rajshri Productions, this project marks Barjatya's return to direction. Further details about the film's plot and additional cast members have yet to be revealed.
